```{r}
library(dplyr)  # bind_rows() comes from dplyr

#merging the location csv files to one dataframe
cherry <- read.csv("data/washingtondc.csv") |> 
  bind_rows(read.csv("data/liestal.csv")) |> 
  bind_rows(read.csv("data/kyoto.csv")) |> 
  bind_rows(read.csv("data/vancouver.csv")) |> 
  bind_rows(read.csv("data/nyc.csv"))
```


```{r}
head(cherry) #looking at the top 6 rows of the new dataframe
```

```{r}

str(cherry) #looking at structure of cherry
summary(cherry) # checking the statistical summary of the cherry dataframe

```


EDA SECTION

```{r}
library(ggplot2)

#creating a scatterplot of cherry bloom dates over the years

ggplot(cherry, aes(x = year, y = bloom_doy, color = location)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_smooth(method = "loess", se = FALSE) +
  labs(title = "Peak bloom trends over time", y = "Day of Year", x = "Year")

```


```{r}

#creating histograms of the distribution of peak bloom days 
#one for each city

ggplot(cherry, aes(x = bloom_doy)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "lightblue") +
  facet_wrap(~location, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of peak bloom days")

```


```{r}
#creates pairwise relationships among bloom timing and geographic/temporal variables in Cherry dataset

pairs(cherry[, c("bloom_doy", "lat", "long", "alt", "year")])

```





```{r}
library(randomForest)

#Random Forest 
#fitting a random forest using year, location, and geographical variables
#then creating a new dataset to hold the peak bloom date predictions


# Make location a factor in your training data
cherry$location <- as.factor(cherry$location)

# Fit the Random Forest
rf_model <- randomForest(
  bloom_doy ~ year + location + lat + long + alt,
  data = cherry,
  ntree = 500
)

# Prepare new data for 2026
new_data <- data.frame(
  year = rep(2026, 5),
  location = c("washingtondc", "liestal", "kyoto", "vancouver", "newyorkcity"),
  lat = c(38.89, 47.48, 35.01, 49.22, 40.73),
  long = c(-77.03, 7.74, 135.67, -123.16, -73.99),
  alt = c(0, 450, 50, 24, 10)
)

# Make sure 'location' is a factor with the same levels as training data
new_data$location <- factor(new_data$location, levels = levels(cherry$location))

# Predict
predictions_doy <- predict(rf_model, newdata = new_data)
predictions_doy

# Convert to calendar dates
doy_to_date <- function(year, doy) {
  as.Date(doy - 1, origin = paste0(year, "-01-01"))
}

predictions_date <- doy_to_date(2026, predictions_doy)
predictions_date

```



```{r}

#visualizing which predictors are most important in the RF
#each bar shows you how much each variable contributes to the model’s predictive power

library(ggplot2)
importance_df <- data.frame(
  feature = rownames(importance(rf_model)),
  importance = importance(rf_model)[,1]
)
ggplot(importance_df, aes(x = reorder(feature, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Random Forest Feature Importance")

```


```{r}
phenometrics <- read.csv("data/USA-NPN_individual_phenometrics_data.csv")

# Look at column names
colnames(phenometrics)

```

```{r}

head(historic_temperatures)
unique(historic_temperatures$location)
unique(cherry$location)
table(historic_temperatures$datatype)
```



using the api and historic temps data to exapnd on my random forest to help with prediction. 

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)

#computing winter temps and merging them with the cherry data
#winter conditions could influence peak bloom date
#need our model to account for climate effects

# Compute average winter temperature (Dec of previous year + Jan/Feb of current year)
#avg_winter_temp <- historic_temperatures %>%
#  filter(datatype == "TMAX") %>%
#  mutate(
#    year_winter = case_when(
  #    month(date) == 12 ~ year(date) + 1,
 #     month(date) %in% c(1,2) ~ year(date),
   #   TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    #)
  #) %>%
  #filter(!is.na(year_winter)) %>%
  #group_by(location, year = year_winter) %>%
  #summarize(avg_winter_tmax = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

#head(avg_winter_temp)


cherry_temp <- cherry %>%
  left_join(avg_winter_tmax, by = c("location", "year"))

summary(cherry_temp$avg_winter_tmax)

```


```{r}
library(tidyr)
#now building a random forest that predicts peak bloom dates but now also considers winter temps
#making the model more climate aware
#helps improve predicting accuracy 


cherry_temp_clean <- cherry_temp %>%
  filter(!is.na(bloom_doy)) %>%
  drop_na(avg_winter_tmax)


#cherry_temp_clean <- cherry_temp %>%
 # filter(
  #  !is.na(bloom_doy),
   # !is.na(avg_winter_tmax),
  #  !is.na(avg_winter_tmin),
   # !is.na(frost_days),
#    #!is.na(lat),
 #   !is.na(long),
  #  !is.na(alt)
#  )
cherry_temp_clean$location <- as.factor(cherry_temp_clean$location)
nrow(cherry_temp_clean)


sum(!is.na(cherry_temp$avg_winter_tmax))

```

```{r}
library(randomForest)

# Make location a factor
cherry_temp$location <- as.factor(cherry_temp$location)

rf_model_temp <- randomForest(
  bloom_doy ~ year + location + lat + long + alt +
    avg_winter_tmax,
  data = cherry_temp_clean,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE
)



rf_model_temp
print(rf_model_temp)



```
#checking importance
```{r}

#checking the importance of each variable again but now also including winter temperatures


importance_df <- data.frame(
  feature = rownames(importance(rf_model_temp)),
  importance = importance(rf_model_temp)[,1]  # %IncMSE for regression
)

importance_df


library(ggplot2)

ggplot(importance_df, aes(x = reorder(feature, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Random Forest Feature Importance",
       x = "Feature",
       y = "% Increase in MSE if permuted")


```

```{r}

############################
# 4. COMPUTE GROWING DEGREE DAYS (GDD)
############################

#calculates daily Growing Degree Days (GDD) for cherry blossoms from historical temperature data

library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(randomForest)

# Base temperature for cherry blossoms
base_temp <- 5  

# Compute daily GDD (heat above base temperature)
gdd_daily <- historic_temperatures %>%
  filter(datatype == "TAVG") %>%
  mutate(
    year = year(date),
    doy = yday(date),
    gdd = pmax(value - base_temp, 0)
  )

############################
# PRESEASON GDD 
# Accumulate heat from Jan 1 to March 15 (DOY 74)
#early season warming can influence when peak bloom happens
############################

gdd_preseason <- gdd_daily %>%
  filter(doy <= 74) %>%   # March 15
  group_by(location, year) %>%
  summarize(gdd_march15 = sum(gdd, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

############################
# MERGE WITH BLOOM DATA
############################
cherry_temp_clean <- cherry_temp %>%
  left_join(gdd_preseason, by = c("location", "year")) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(bloom_doy),
    !is.na(avg_winter_tmax),
    !is.na(gdd_march15),
    !is.na(lat),
    !is.na(long),
    !is.na(alt)
  )
cherry_temp_clean$location <- as.factor(cherry_temp_clean$location)

############################
# 5. RANDOM FOREST WITH WINTER TEMP + PRESEASON GDD
############################

rf_model_temp_gdd <- randomForest(
  bloom_doy ~ year + location + lat + long + alt +
    avg_winter_tmax +
    gdd_march15,
  data = cherry_temp_clean,
  ntree = 500,
  importance = TRUE
)

# View model summary
print(rf_model_temp_gdd)

# View model summary
rf_model_temp_gdd

```
```{r}
############################
# 6. FEATURE IMPORTANCE
############################

importance_df <- data.frame(
  feature = rownames(importance(rf_model_temp_gdd)),
  importance = importance(rf_model_temp_gdd)[,1]  # %IncMSE
)

importance_df

ggplot(importance_df, aes(x = reorder(feature, importance), y = importance)) +
  geom_col(fill = "skyblue") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Random Forest Feature Importance",
       x = "Feature",
       y = "% Increase in MSE if permuted")


```

```{r}
importance(rf_model_temp_gdd)
varImpPlot(rf_model_temp_gdd)

```

















new idea
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(randomForest)
library(ggplot2)

predict_peak_bloom <- function(target_year, cherry_data, rf_model, historic_temperatures, base_temp = 5, chill_threshold = 7) {
  
  # --- 1. Get location info ---
  geo_info <- cherry_data %>%
    distinct(location, lat, long, alt)
  
  # --- 2. Compute winter TMAX for target_year (Dec–Feb) ---
  winter_tmax <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype == "TMAX") %>%
    mutate(year_winter = case_when(
      month(date) == 12 ~ year(date) + 1,
      month(date) %in% c(1,2) ~ year(date),
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    )) %>%
    filter(year_winter == target_year) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(avg_winter_tmax = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  # --- 2b. Compute winter chill days (Dec–Feb, TAVG < chill_threshold) ---
  winter_chill <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype == "TAVG") %>%
    mutate(year_winter = case_when(
      month(date) == 12 ~ year(date) + 1,
      month(date) %in% c(1,2) ~ year(date),
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    )) %>%
    filter(year_winter == target_year) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(winter_chill_days = sum(value < chill_threshold, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  # --- 3. Compute pre-season GDD (Jan 1 – Mar 15) ---
  gdd_preseason <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype == "TAVG") %>%
    mutate(year = year(date),
           doy = yday(date),
           gdd = pmax(value - base_temp, 0)) %>%
    filter(year == target_year, doy <= 74) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(gdd_march15 = sum(gdd, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  # --- 3b. Compute GDD Mar 1–15 ---
  gdd_mar1_15 <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype == "TAVG") %>%
    mutate(year = year(date),
           doy = yday(date),
           gdd = pmax(value - base_temp, 0)) %>%
    filter(year == target_year, doy >= 60 & doy <= 74) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(gdd_mar1_15 = sum(gdd, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  # --- 4. Compute avg spring (Mar–Apr, TAVG/TMAX) ---
  avg_spring <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype %in% c("TAVG", "TMAX"), month(date) %in% c(3,4)) %>%
    mutate(year = year(date)) %>%
    filter(year == target_year) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(avg_spring = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  # --- 5. Combine predictors ---
  newdata <- geo_info %>%
    left_join(winter_tmax, by = "location") %>%
    left_join(winter_chill, by = "location") %>%
    left_join(gdd_preseason, by = "location") %>%
    left_join(gdd_mar1_15, by = "location") %>%
    left_join(avg_spring, by = "location") %>%
    mutate(
      year = target_year,
      location = factor(location, levels = levels(cherry_data$location)),
      lat = as.numeric(lat),
      long = as.numeric(long),
      alt = as.numeric(alt),
      avg_winter_tmax = as.numeric(avg_winter_tmax),
      gdd_march15 = as.numeric(gdd_march15),
      gdd_mar1_15 = as.numeric(gdd_mar1_15),
      avg_spring = as.numeric(avg_spring),
      winter_chill_days = as.numeric(winter_chill_days)
    )
  
  # --- 6. Fill missing values with historical averages ---
  # Historical means
  hist_tmax <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype == "TMAX") %>%
    mutate(year_winter = case_when(
      month(date) == 12 ~ year(date) + 1,
      month(date) %in% c(1,2) ~ year(date),
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    )) %>%
    filter(!is.na(year_winter)) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(hist_avg_winter_tmax = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  hist_gdd <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype == "TAVG") %>%
    mutate(year = year(date), doy = yday(date), gdd = pmax(value - base_temp, 0)) %>%
    filter(doy <= 74) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(hist_gdd_march15 = mean(gdd, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  hist_spring <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype %in% c("TAVG","TMAX"), month(date) %in% c(3,4)) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(hist_avg_spring = mean(value, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  hist_gdd_mar1_15 <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype == "TAVG") %>%
    mutate(doy = yday(date), gdd = pmax(value - base_temp, 0)) %>%
    filter(doy >= 60 & doy <= 74) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(hist_gdd_mar1_15 = mean(gdd, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")
  
  hist_chill <- historic_temperatures %>%
    filter(datatype == "TAVG") %>%
    mutate(year_winter = case_when(
      month(date) == 12 ~ year(date) + 1,
      month(date) %in% c(1,2) ~ year(date),
      TRUE ~ NA_integer_
    )) %>%
    filter(!is.na(year_winter)) %>%
    group_by(location) %>%
    summarize(hist_winter_chill_days = sum(value < chill_threshold, na.rm = TRUE)/n_distinct(year_winter), .groups = "drop")
  
  # Fill NAs
  newdata <- newdata %>%
    left_join(hist_tmax, by = "location") %>%
    left_join(hist_gdd, by = "location") %>%
    left_join(hist_spring, by = "location") %>%
    left_join(hist_gdd_mar1_15, by = "location") %>%
    left_join(hist_chill, by = "location") %>%
    mutate(
      avg_winter_tmax = ifelse(is.na(avg_winter_tmax), hist_avg_winter_tmax, avg_winter_tmax),
      gdd_march15 = ifelse(is.na(gdd_march15), hist_gdd_march15, gdd_march15),
      gdd_mar1_15 = ifelse(is.na(gdd_mar1_15), hist_gdd_mar1_15, gdd_mar1_15),
      avg_spring = ifelse(is.na(avg_spring), hist_avg_spring, avg_spring),
      winter_chill_days = ifelse(is.na(winter_chill_days), hist_winter_chill_days, winter_chill_days)
    ) %>%
    select(-hist_avg_winter_tmax, -hist_gdd_march15, -hist_avg_spring, -hist_gdd_mar1_15, -hist_winter_chill_days)
  
  # --- 7. Ensure factor levels match training data ---
  newdata$location <- factor(newdata$location, levels = levels(cherry_data$location))
  
  # --- 8. Predict DOY and convert to date ---
  predicted_doy <- predict(rf_model, newdata = newdata)
  
  predictions <- newdata %>%
    select(location) %>%
    mutate(
      year = target_year,
      prediction = round(predicted_doy),
      predicted_date = as.Date(prediction - 1, origin = paste0(year, "-01-01"))
    )
  
  return(predictions)
}

# Example usage for 2026
predictions_2026 <- predict_peak_bloom(
  target_year = 2026,
  cherry_data = cherry_temp_clean,
  rf_model = rf_model_temp_gdd,
  historic_temperatures = historic_temperatures
)

predictions_2026
```
